assertions.title: Dashboard header buttons (Recover, Export, Policies, Settings) are enabled and clickable
assertions.reason: All four header actions were present, had no disabled attribute/disabled property, and each click produced its expected action.

steps.action: Inspect Recover/Export/Policies/Settings buttons on dashboard header
steps.expected: Buttons exist and are enabled without disabled attribute
steps.observed: All four buttons exist with hasDisabledAttr=false and disabledProp=false
Export modal opened with password fields and controls

evidence.network: none captured by agent-browser (client-side route/modal actions)

assertions.title: Settings gear opens slide-over sidebar with all sections
assertions.reason: Clicking the gear opened a right-side settings panel with all required sections and an active scrim over dashboard content.

steps.expected: Slide-over sidebar opens with required sections and scrim
steps.observed: Sidebar opened with Device Profile, Relays, Group Profile, Rotate Share, Export & Backup, Profile Security; scrim candidate overlay present

evidence.network: none captured by agent-browser (UI-only sidebar open)

assertions.title: Sidebar close button (X) dismisses sidebar and removes scrim
assertions.reason: Close settings (X) dismissed the panel and returned to fully interactive dashboard view.

steps.observed: Sidebar removed; dashboard-only interactive controls remained

assertions.title: Lock Profile button in sidebar navigates to Welcome (/)
assertions.reason: Lock from Profile Security returned to / with Welcome screen and no active dashboard.

steps.action: Open settings sidebar and click Profile Security Lock
steps.expected: Navigate to / Welcome and close sidebar
steps.observed: Navigated to / and saw Welcome with unlockable profile row

assertions.title: Clear Credentials button opens destructive confirmation modal with red styling
assertions.reason: Clicking Clear opened confirmation modal with destructive red confirm styling and warning copy.

steps.observed: Modal opened with Clear Credentials title, warning text, and red confirm button (rgb(220, 38, 38))

evidence.network: none captured by agent-browser (UI-only modal open)

assertions.title: Clear Credentials Confirm clears data and navigates to Welcome, Cancel dismisses modal
assertions.reason: Cancel kept user on dashboard/sidebar without data loss; confirm removed saved profile and returned to first-time Welcome; no console errors observed.

steps.action: Open Clear Credentials modal and click Cancel
steps.observed: Modal dismissed, remained at /dashboard/{profileId} with settings sidebar still open

steps.action: Open Clear Credentials modal and click red Clear Credentials confirm
steps.expected: Credentials cleared and navigate to first-time Welcome
steps.observed: Navigated to / with first-time Welcome variant (no profile row)

steps.action: Check console errors during cancel/confirm paths

evidence.network: none captured by agent-browser (client-side modal/navigation flow)

assertions.title: Sidebar scrim blocks dashboard interaction beneath it
assertions.reason: Clicking scrim area closed sidebar without triggering underlying dashboard actions.

steps.expected: Dashboard controls behind scrim are blocked
steps.observed: Scrim overlay active while sidebar open

steps.action: Click left-side scrim area over dashboard
steps.expected: Sidebar closes only; no dashboard action triggered
steps.observed: Sidebar closed and dashboard remained in Running state (no Recover/Stop action fired)

evidence.network: none captured by agent-browser (UI-only overlay interaction)

frictions.description: To create isolated dashboard state, Finish Distribution was initially disabled until both remote share QR modals were opened and dismissed in Create > Distribute Shares.
frictions.resolution: Opened each QR modal and clicked Done, then Finish Distribution became enabled.

frictions.description: agent-browser network capture returned no requests for these client-side UI transitions/modals.
frictions.resolution: Used screenshot + URL + console evidence as primary signal for each assertion.

summary: Tested 7 assertions (VAL-DASH-011, VAL-SETTINGS-001..006): 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
